text:
Once upon a time in the quiet town of Willow Creek, there was an old oak tree near the center park. Animals knew each trunk bight as a friend.

One autumn afternoon, a curious squirrel named Rusty decided to hang upside-down from an ancient branch. The wind blew through the leaves, singing a gentle nursery rhyme. Rusty thought the words were about stars twinkling in heaven.

As he listened, he realized they were actually an ode to old friendship. "Their hearts fly eastward," he imagined they were saying.

Rusty loved those old oaks. They were wise like old books and knew ancient secrets.

One evening, a soft rain fell, turning the world into a velvet dream map. Rusty nestled warmly beneath the thick carpet of leaves. In front of him, two wise owl brothers sat silently.

"They haven't spoken for moonlit nights," Rusty thought.

One brother spoke softly. "Our friend Oak."

"Yes," said the other. "Just listen."
